---
title: "Policy and Value Functions in a HANK Model"
subtitle: "Computational Economics Final Project"
author:
  - name: Lorenzo Castro
    affiliations: Collegio Carlo Alberto
  - name: Paolo Diotallevi
    affiliations: Collegio Carlo Alberto
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: true
    toc-location: right
    number-sections: false
    fig-cap-location: bottom
    fig-align: center
    smooth-scroll: true
    page-layout: article
output-file: report.html
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

# Overview

This report studies policy and value functions in a HANK-style model. The main goal is to generate and visualize model-based objects such as consumption, labor, savings, value functions, and flow utility.

The project is implemented in Julia. The figures reported below are generated directly from the model code contained in this repository.

# Project repository

The Julia project repository is available at:

[https://github.com/LorenzoCastroDeSalvo/final_project_CE](https://github.com/LorenzoCastroDeSalvo/final_project_CE)

The online report is available at:

[https://lorenzocastrodesalvo.github.io/final_project_CE/](https://lorenzocastrodesalvo.github.io/final_project_CE/)

# Replication goals

The goal of the project is to create a small computational package that generates and visualizes the main policy and value functions of the model.

More specifically, the project aims to:

- implement the model objects in Julia;
- generate the main policy-function and value-function figures;
- save the figures in reproducible output folders;
- produce a Quarto report that documents the computational results.

# Model summary

The project focuses on the numerical analysis of policy and value functions in a heterogeneous-agent New Keynesian model.

The main objects studied in the report are consumption, labor, savings, value functions, and flow utility. These objects describe how agents behave in the model and how the model solution changes across different asset levels and cash-on-hand values.

The reported objects are not obtained from raw data. They are generated directly from the model and from the Julia scripts included in the repository.

# Julia implementation

The core model code is stored in:

```text
src/HANKPolicies.jl
```

The main figures are generated by:

```text
scripts/hank-figures.jl
```

The supplementary diagnostic figures are generated by:

```text
scripts/policy_value_plots.jl
```

The project uses `Project.toml` and `Manifest.toml` to define the Julia environment and make the computational results reproducible.

# Repository structure

The repository is organized as follows:

```text
src/HANKPolicies.jl                 Core Julia module
scripts/hank-figures.jl             Generates the main report figures
scripts/policy_value_plots.jl       Generates supplementary diagnostics
figures/figure1.png                 Main report Figure 1
figures/figure1.pdf                 PDF version of Figure 1
figures/figure2.png                 Main report Figure 2
figures/figure2.pdf                 PDF version of Figure 2
output/*.png                        Supplementary generated diagnostics
report.qmd                          Quarto source file for the report
report.html                         Rendered HTML report
```

# Main results

The two figures in this section are the main report outputs. They are loaded directly from the `figures/` directory. The corresponding `.pdf` files are also available in the same folder for publication or LaTeX workflows.

![Main model-generated report figure.](figures/figure1.png){#fig-figure1 width="72%"}

![Second main model-generated report figure.](figures/figure2.png){#fig-figure2 width="72%"}

# Policy functions

The figures below are generated by `scripts/policy_value_plots.jl` and saved in `output/`.

They show how the policy functions vary with assets and cash-on-hand. These plots are useful for checking whether the model solution behaves in a reasonable way across the relevant state space.

::: {layout-ncol=2}
![Consumption policy by assets.](output/policy_consumption_vs_assets.png){#fig-cons-assets}

![Consumption policy by cash-on-hand.](output/policy_consumption_vs_cash_on_hand.png){#fig-cons-coh}

![Labor policy by assets.](output/policy_labor_vs_assets.png){#fig-labor-assets}

![Labor policy by cash-on-hand.](output/policy_labor_vs_cash_on_hand.png){#fig-labor-coh}
:::

The next figure reports the savings policy as a function of assets.

![Savings policy by assets.](output/policy_savings_vs_assets.png){#fig-savings-assets width="70%"}

# Value and utility

The final diagnostic figures report the value function and flow utility. These figures provide additional checks on the shape of the model solution.

::: {layout-ncol=2}
![Value function by assets.](output/value_function_vs_assets.png){#fig-value-assets}

![Flow utility by assets.](output/flow_utility_vs_assets.png){#fig-flow-assets}
:::

# Reproducibility

The Julia environment is defined by `Project.toml` and `Manifest.toml`.

To reproduce the outputs from the repository root, run:

```bash
julia --project=. -e 'using Pkg; Pkg.instantiate()'
julia --project=. scripts/hank-figures.jl
julia --project=. scripts/policy_value_plots.jl
```

To render the report locally, run:

```bash
quarto render report.qmd
```

# Conclusion

This project provides a reproducible Julia implementation for generating policy-function and value-function figures in a HANK-style model. The report documents the main outputs of the computational project and provides the commands needed to reproduce the results.
